lts.lt_definition: I can explain place value, prime factorisation, and relationships between different number forms including fractions, decimals, and percentages.
I can apply the four operations, order of operations, powers, roots, and standard form to calculate with integers, fractions, decimals, and negative numbers.
I can use standard units of measurement and round numbers to appropriate degrees of accuracy.
I can use approximation to estimate answers and calculate possible errors using inequality notation.
I can use calculators and other technologies to calculate accurately and understand the infinite nature of number sets.
I can use and interpret algebraic notation including coefficients, powers, brackets, and standard conventions.
I can simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions by collecting terms, expanding brackets, and factoring.
I can work with coordinates, sketch graphs of functions, and manipulate linear equations to standard form.
I can analyze gradients and intercepts, estimate values from graphs, and solve problems using graphical representations.
I can generate terms of a sequence using term-to-term or position-to-term rules.
I can recognize arithmetic and geometric sequences and find their nth terms.

lts.competency_name: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change
lts.lt_name: Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships
lts.lt_definition: I can use ratios, fractions, and proportional reasoning to solve problems involving multiplicative relationships between quantities.

lt_names: Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships
Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships
Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

lts.competency_name: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change
lts.lt_definition: I can solve problems involving rates, percentage changes, and compound units in real-world contexts.

lt_names: Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships
Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships
Working with Ratios and Proportional Relationships

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

lts.competency_name: Measurement and Calculation of Geometric Properties
lts.lt_definition: I can calculate perimeters and areas of triangles, parallelograms, trapezia, circles, and composite shapes.

lts.competency_name: Measurement and Calculation of Geometric Properties
lts.lt_definition: I can calculate volumes of cuboids, cubes, and other prisms including cylinders.
I can draw and measure line segments and angles in geometric figures using conventional methods and interpret scale drawings.
I can perform standard ruler and compass constructions and apply the concept of perpendicular distance from a point to a line.
I can describe, sketch and draw geometric shapes using conventional terms and notations.

lts.competency_name: Properties and Relationships of Geometric Figures
lts.lt_definition: I can identify and apply properties of triangles, quadrilaterals, and circles, including congruence criteria.

lts.competency_name: Properties and Relationships of Geometric Figures
lts.lt_definition: I can construct congruent triangles and create similar shapes through enlargement using various methods.
I can identify properties of and describe the results of translations, rotations and reflections applied to given figures.
I can use the properties of faces, surfaces, edges and vertices of three-dimensional shapes to solve problems.
I can apply properties of angles at points, on straight lines, with parallel lines, and in polygons to solve problems.

lt_names: Geometric Proof and Advanced Problem Solving
Geometric Proof and Advanced Problem Solving

lts.lt_name: Geometric Proof and Advanced Problem Solving
lts.lt_definition: I can use geometric theorems, congruence, similarity, and trigonometric relationships to derive results and construct proofs.

lt_names: Geometric Proof and Advanced Problem Solving
Geometric Proof and Advanced Problem Solving

lts.lt_definition: I can record, describe and analyse the frequency of outcomes in probability experiments using appropriate language and the 0-1 probability scale.
I can enumerate sets and their unions and intersections systematically using tables and grids.
I can describe, interpret and compare distributions of single variables using appropriate graphs and measures of central tendency and spread.
I can construct and interpret appropriate tables, charts and diagrams for categorical and numerical data.
I can describe mathematical relationships between two variables and illustrate them using scatter graphs.
